steps.title: Set off the method name
steps.content: The first sentence already identifies the scientists' method. In the next sentence, called ELISA adds the method's name as extra information and should be set off with commas, matching the comma already after the parenthetical definition.

steps.title: Reject mismatched punctuation
steps.content: Choice A opens a parenthetical structure that is not properly balanced. Choice B opens with a dash but closes with a comma, creating mismatched punctuation. Choice D fails to set off the extra naming phrase. The answer is C.
